title: Iran parliament prepares law to set conditions for transit through Strait of Hormuz — Israeli ships banned
snippet: Iran Deputy Speaker Ali Nikzad announced the parliament is advancing legislation to impose a 'new legal regime' on the Strait of Hormuz. Key provisions: Israeli ships permanently banned; hostile countries (including US) denied passage; all other ships require Iranian permission; tolls charged for safety/guidance/environmental services; violators face ship seizure and 20% cargo confiscation. Revenues allocated to defense, livelihoods, and reconstruction. Nikzad claims the law respects international law and neighboring countries' rights.
tag_reason: New legislative development — parliament advancing formal law to codify Hormuz control, not previously reported
